title: GitLab CI/CD
content:
### GitLab CI/CD

GitLab CI is GitLab's built-in CI/CD system. It is configured with a `.gitlab-ci.yml` file at the root of your repository.

### `.gitlab-ci.yml` Structure

```yaml
stages:
  - build
  - test
  - deploy

build-job:
  stage: build
  script:
    - echo "Building..."
    - npm install
    - npm run build

test-job:
  stage: test
  script:
    - npm test

deploy-job:
  stage: deploy
  script:
    - echo "Deploying..."
```

### Stages

Jobs are grouped into **stages**. All jobs in a stage run in parallel. The next stage starts only when all jobs in the current stage succeed.

### Jobs

Each job has a unique name and belongs to a stage. Jobs define what commands to run and can specify additional configuration.

### `before_script`

Commands that run before every job or all jobs in a pipeline:

```yaml
before_script:
  - npm ci
  - echo "Running on $CI_RUNNER_EXECUTABLE_ARCH"
```

### Artifacts

Files and directories passed between stages:

```yaml
build-job:
  stage: build
  artifacts:
    paths:
      - dist/
    expire_in: 1 week
```

### Cache

Reuse dependency directories across pipelines to speed up builds:

```yaml
cache:
  key: $CI_COMMIT_REF_SLUG
  paths:
    - node_modules/
```

### GitLab Runners

Runners execute CI jobs. GitLab offers shared runners (free tier with limits) and you can install your own specific runners on any machine.

### Docker Execution

Jobs can run inside Docker containers:

```yaml
build-job:
  image: node:20
  script:
    - npm ci
    - npm run build
```

### Key Differences from GitHub Actions

- Uses `stages` with sequential progression
- Jobs in same stage run in parallel
- `.gitlab-ci.yml` at repository root (not `.github/workflows/`)
- Built-in container registry and artifact management
codeExample:
stages:
  - build
  - test
  - deploy

variables:
  NODE_ENV: production

before_script:
  - npm ci

cache:
  key: ${CI_COMMIT_REF_SLUG}
  paths:
    - node_modules/

build:
  stage: build
  image: node:20
  script:
    - npm run build
  artifacts:
    paths:
      - dist/

test:
  stage: test
  image: node:20
  script:
    - npm test

deploy:
  stage: deploy
  image: alpine:latest
  script:
    - echo "Deploying application"
  only:
    - main

quiz.question: What file configures GitLab CI/CD pipelines?
In GitLab CI, when does the next stage begin?
options: Only when all jobs in the current stage succeed
When a manual trigger is clicked
